economic_notes: First Nation derives 68% of revenue from ISC and Province of BC. Partnerships include Sema:th Xo:tsa Developments LP (land development), Sth'oqi Aquaculture LP (aquaculture), Kilgard Business Park LP (commercial), Wochmel Security LP (security), and Sema:th Management Services LP. Settlement Trust holds Trans Mountain pipeline settlement proceeds. $3,000,000 in unspent Trans Mountain relationship agreement funds.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2023. Consolidated statements include the Operating Fund, Capital Fund, Social Housing Fund, Upper Sumas IR6 Right-of-Way Settlement Trust, and Sumas First Nation Minors' Trust. Four subsidiaries accounted for using modified equity method: Sumas Clay Products Ltd. (100%), SFN Renewable Energy Ltd. (100%), Sema:th Xot:sa Developments Ltd. (100%), and 1099212 B.C. Ltd. (50%). Three GBEs accounted for using modified equity method: SXDLP, SALP, and KBPLP. No schedule of remuneration was included in the document. Expenses capitalized of $5,866,083 netted in expense schedule. ISC funding includes both direct ISC ($8,844,638) and ISC contracts for service ($7,585,539). Total government transfers calculated as ISC + ISC contracts + Province of BC + CMHC + First Nations Health Authority + Dept of Natural Resources + FNESC = $8,844,638 + $7,585,539 + $2,922,273 + $2,007,594 + $607,270 + $399,034 + $226,579 + $49,315 + $4,116 = $22,646,358 approximately. OSR includes lease/other income, property taxation, gaming, Sto:lo, commercial enterprises, City of Abbotsford, FNLMRC, trust income, partnership losses, and investment gains/losses.
